subject: Mathematics
title: The Proof of Fermat's Last Theorem
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Proof of Fermat's Last Theorem.

hook.text: Fermat claimed no proof fit his margin. 358 years later, Andrew Wiles filled 129 pages with one.

buildup.text: Wiles worked in secret for seven years, telling almost no one about his quest.

discovery.text: He connected Fermat's theorem to elliptic curves and modular forms—a link nobody had thought possible.

twist.text: His first proof had a subtle error. It took another year of agonizing work to fix it.

climax.text: The corrected proof was published in 1995. Wiles received a special Fields Medal and a knighthood.

punchline.text: 358 years, 129 pages, and one very patient mathematician.

quiz.question: How long did Andrew Wiles work secretly on Fermat's Last Theorem?
